title: Conditions and switch

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Conditions and switch; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: if, switch, and conditional expressions choose paths using truthiness or strict case matching.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A nullish check distinguishes missing count from a legitimate zero. Your goal is to implement and test boundary-complete conditional logic. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Values, types and expressions; pf abstraction calls; Equality, truthiness and coercion. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: if, switch, and conditional expressions choose paths using truthiness or strict case matching. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Falsy values include 0, empty string, null, undefined, false, and NaN, while empty arrays are truthy. This draft therefore rejects: An empty array is falsy because it contains no elements.

includes: Write complete branches with deliberate truth tests and lexical scoping.
Implement and test boundary-complete conditional logic.
excludes: Falsy values include 0, empty string, null, undefined, false, and NaN, while empty arrays are truthy.

outcomes: Implement and test boundary-complete conditional logic.

examples.title: Conditions and switch: concrete case
examples.situation: A nullish check distinguishes missing count from a legitimate zero.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A nullish check distinguishes missing count from a legitimate zero.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Arrays and objects are truthy even when empty; test length or the intended condition explicitly.
examples.result: The result follows because Arrays and objects are truthy even when empty; test length or the intended condition explicitly.

examples.title: Conditions and switch: boundary case
examples.situation: Falsy values include 0, empty string, null, undefined, false, and NaN, while empty arrays are truthy.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Falsy values include 0, empty string, null, undefined, false, and NaN, while empty arrays are truthy.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: An empty array is falsy because it contains no elements.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Arrays and objects are truthy even when empty; test length or the intended condition explicitly.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: An empty array is falsy because it contains no elements.
misconceptions.correction: Arrays and objects are truthy even when empty; test length or the intended condition explicitly.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: An empty array is falsy because it contains no elements.

analogy.example: A coordinated event desk can model Conditions and switch, with values, tasks, callbacks, and external messages changing observable state.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Conditions and switch before using exact platform terms
limitations: Human coordination does not reproduce coercion, lexical scope, microtasks, prototypes, or browser security boundaries.

recap.summary: You should now be able to implement and test boundary-complete conditional logic. Keep this boundary in view: Falsy values include 0, empty string, null, undefined, false, and NaN, while empty arrays are truthy.
